title: Uncertainty Quantification for LLM Function-Calling
gist: An Apple/University of Oxford paper (with Yarin Gal) presents what it says is the first evaluation of uncertainty quantification (UQ) methods specifically for LLM function-calling/tool-use, finding that multi-sample methods like Semantic Entropy lose their edge over simple single-sample methods in this setting, and proposing FC-specific fixes (AST-based clustering, semantic token filtering) to improve both.

themes.theme: Why UQ matters for agentic tool use

insights.claim: LLMs deployed to autonomously call functions/tools can produce severe, sometimes irreversible harm from incorrect calls (e.g., transferring money or deleting data), making it critical to quantify the model's confidence before a function call is executed.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Frames uncertainty quantification as a prerequisite safety gate for autonomous agent products (payments, file/data operations); any AI-agent platform lacking a pre-execution confidence check carries elevated operational/liability risk.
insights.quote: an LLM calling functions incorrectly can have severe implications, especially when their effects are irreversible, e.g., transferring money or deleting data

themes.theme: Multi-sample UQ loses its edge in function-calling

insights.claim: In the study, presented as the first evaluation of UQ methods for LLM function-calling, multi-sample UQ methods such as Semantic Entropy -- which perform strongly on natural-language Q&A -- show no clear advantage over simple single-sample UQ methods when applied to function-calling outputs.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Undercuts the assumption that the best QA-domain UQ techniques generalize to agentic tool-calling; teams building confidence-gated agents should not default to expensive multi-sample methods without FC-specific validation.
insights.quote: we find that in the FC setting, it offers no clear advantage over simple single-sample UQ methods

insights.claim: Multi-sample UQ methods for function-calling can be improved by clustering the sampled outputs based on their abstract syntax tree (AST) parsing rather than raw text similarity.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: A concrete, implementable technique for teams building tool-calling agents to boost confidence-estimation accuracy without switching UQ paradigms.
insights.quote: multi-sample UQ methods benefit from clustering FC outputs based on their abstract syntax tree parsing

insights.claim: Single-sample UQ methods can be improved for function-calling by restricting logit-based uncertainty scoring to only semantically meaningful tokens, rather than scoring the full token sequence.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Offers a low-cost (single forward pass) confidence-scoring improvement that avoids the latency/compute overhead of multi-sample approaches -- relevant for on-device or latency-sensitive agent deployments like Apple's.
insights.quote: single-sample UQ methods can be improved by selecting only semantically meaningful tokens when calculating logit-based uncertainty scores

themes.theme: Evaluation methodology risk in the broader UQ field

insights.claim: A related Apple/Oxford paper on UQ evaluation warns that 'mutual biases' -- where both the UQ method and the correctness metric (e.g., ROUGE-L) are distorted by the same underlying factors -- can systematically bias standard evaluations like AUROC.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: A methodological caution: reported UQ benchmark scores across the industry may overstate real-world reliability if evaluation and correctness metrics share hidden biases -- worth discounting vendor UQ claims that rely solely on AUROC-style benchmarks.
insights.quote: mutual biases—when both UQ methods and correctness functions are biased by the same factors—systematically distort evaluation
insights.locator: Related paper teaser, June 2025 ACL entry

insights.claim: Multi-sample UQ methods such as Semantic Entropy typically require 5-10 inference calls per query, a computational cost cited as a motivation for finding cheaper alternatives for safety-critical LLM deployment.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Quantifies the inference-cost multiplier (5-10x) that multi-sample safety gating imposes, a real unit-economics consideration for any agentic product that wants to check confidence before every action.
insights.quote: multi-sample methods such as Semantic Entropy (Kuhn et al., 2023) usually require 5-10 inference
insights.locator: Related paper teaser, November 2024 NeurIPS workshop entry
